name: Pattern Matching
def: Control flow that matches a value against structural patterns and binds parts of it.
problem: Branch on the shape/variant of data and destructure it safely and exhaustively.
mechanism: Patterns match variants, tuples, and nested fields, binding sub-values; exhaustiveness checking forces handling every case.
benefits: Exhaustiveness catches unhandled cases at compile time
Pairs perfectly with algebraic data types
limits: Limited or absent in C/older Java
failures: Non-exhaustive matches with catch-all hiding new variants
myths: "Just a switch statement" — it also destructures and checks exhaustiveness
